main_dirt.bias_notes: Counter-Clockwise. South Australia's premier dirt track. The surface is a unique sand/dirt mix that creates severe kickback. The 1,600m circumference makes the turns relatively tight, but it features a generous 400m home straight. Because the kickback is so blinding, horses making their debut on this surface often panic and drop the bit entirely.
main_dirt.post_bias: Inside barriers (1-4) hold a distinct advantage, but *only* if the horse possesses the early gate speed to clear the field. A horse drawn in barrier 1 that misses the start will be completely buried in the kickback.

handicapping_angles.the_dirt_specialist: SPECIALIST TRACK. Proven dirt form is absolutely critical here. Heavily upgrade tough, older country horses with multiple wins at Port Augusta. Conversely, apply extreme caution to high-priced horses shipping in from turf tracks for the first time.
handicapping_angles.kickback_survival: Because the kickback is so severe, horses that can lead or sit just off the pace *outside* of the kickback hold a massive advantage. Downgrade timid stalkers drawn inside who lack early speed.
handicapping_angles.regional_form_penalty: This is deep regional dirt form. If you are evaluating a horse whose past performances are built at Port Augusta, you must apply the maximum class penalty if they are shipping down to turf Metro venues like Morphettville.
